subject: [UUI] Update image of components of UUI

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > images have been given far after M3.
> > Will wait for honolulu branching to merge
> 
> Hi,
> We have already created honolulu branch for UUI, and also we have changed the codes and self released new version after M3. What should we do?

The problem is that this is the first container image that you are releasing in Honolulu so I expect it's not a minor bugfix but a major update compared to Guilin. Deadline for creating review with new containers was at M3 which was 4 weeks ago which means that you are 4 weeks after the deadline.

Just to be clear, we are not against this patch. If it works then as soon as we branch out Honoulu (probably on Monday) we'll merge it to the master branch and it's just going to become a part of Istanbul release
Hashtag added: istanbul

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Approved the version change

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

As noted earlier. This patch has been provided four (*4*) weeks after the deadline. With all my hart I try to support each and every project team that updates their charts in OOM including UUI team. Never the less, in the same time I believe it's highly unfair for other project teams who are playing by the rules to give some other team additional month for the development. In my opinion rules should be followed by everyone otherwise there is no point to have any rules.

We had plenty of late coming patches but those were late a day or maximum a week after M3 not a month. So where is the border? Will we accept new features even a day before sign-off?

As TSC is a body which sets rules in ONAP I'd prefer to have a TSC decision if this is supposed to be an exception.

Patch Set 4:

@Krzysztof; this change maybe arrived after M3 but typically we can still update the image version until RC2. In the new release cadence, can the project only do it at the M3? If it is the case then my apologies I have not yet captured this change. We should communicate to the PTLS on Monday 3/29 because I am not sure if all of them are aware of this change versus what we did before.

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4:
> 
> @Krzysztof; this change maybe arrived after M3 but typically we can still update the image version until RC2. In the new release cadence, can the project only do it at the M3? If it is the case then my apologies I have not yet captured this change. We should communicate to the PTLS on Monday 3/29 because I am not sure if all of them are aware of this change versus what we did before.

@Catherine
It's perfectly fine to provide bugfixes after M3. It's human to err and make bugs but in my opinion this is not a bugfix. It's a first update of this container that we are seeing in this release so I expect it to be a functional change not just a bugfix update of the container
